content.full_text:
February 21, 1990

MariaTheresa Viramontes  
Office of Assemblymember Robert J. Campbell  
State Capitol  
P.O. Box 942849  
Sacramento, CA 94249-0001

Re: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. A-90-051

Dear Ms. Viramontes:

This will confirm telephone advice provided to you on January 11, 1990. You were advised that payment for a computer for the district office could be made from Assemblymember Campbell’s officeholder account in order to avoid repossession of the computer while awaiting funds for the purchase from the Assembly Rules Committee. The officeholder account could then be reimbursed from his assembly account when the funds were made available.

The Political Reform Act (the "Act") governs appropriate use of campaign funds (Sections 85800 - 85807). Purchasing a computer for the district office is a permissible use of campaign funds in that the expenditure is reasonably related to a legislative or governmental purpose. (Section 85801.) However, legal title to the computer must not reside, in whole or in part, in a candidate, elected officer, member of his or her immediate family, or the treasurer of the committee. (Section 85805(a).) Since the computer is for the district office and is ultimately to be paid for with public funds, it is my understanding that legal title to the computer resides with the State of California.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

Our File No. A-90-051
Page 2

If you have any questions regarding the above, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

[Signature]
By: Margaret W. Ellison
Counsel, Legal Division

KED/MWE/aa
